articles.title: Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground
articles.description:
Prosecutors reportedly said in court Friday that a witness saw the father and son playing soccer and discussing the World Cup prior to the stabbing.

The post Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Wellesley man James Bennett pleads not guilty to fatally stabbing his father at a playground.
Witnesses reported seeing them playing soccer and discussing the World Cup before the incident.
The incident raises concerns about safety in local parks for Boston area families.

articles.title: Small plane crashes into tallest building in Beijing
articles.description:
Video footage showed broken windows and debris, including what appeared to be the tail of a small aircraft, falling dozens of stories into the street below.

The post Small plane crashes into tallest building in Beijing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Small plane crashed into Beijing's tallest building, causing broken windows and debris.
Video footage showed debris falling dozens of stories onto the street below.
Boston residents should stay informed about global aviation safety and potential impacts.

articles.title: Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester
articles.description:
Police say the 4-year-old is expected to recover as the incident remains under investigation.

The post Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A 4-year-old boy fell from a second-floor window in Worcester.
The child is hospitalized but expected to recover, according to police.
The incident is under investigation, raising safety concerns for local families.

articles.title: 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill
articles.description:
Police responded to the intersection of Portland Street and 5th Avenue in Haverhill Thursday afternoon.

The post 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A bus collided with a motorized scooter in Haverhill, resulting in a fatality.
A 12-year-old child died, and another child was seriously injured in the incident.
The accident occurred at the intersection of Portland Street and 5th Avenue on Thursday.

articles.title: Renovating Cambridge’s historic Asa Gray House: How we did it
articles.description:
The 7,588-square-foot residence built in 1810 is a designated National Historic Landmark

The post Renovating Cambridge’s historic Asa Gray House: How we did it appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Renovation of the 7,588-square-foot Asa Gray House in Cambridge completed successfully.
The house, built in 1810, is a designated National Historic Landmark.
Restoration enhances local heritage and attracts visitors to Cambridge's historic sites.

articles.title: Massachusetts House passes bill safeguarding libraries from book bans
articles.description:
The bill gives school librarians primary authority over selecting library materials.

The post Massachusetts House passes bill safeguarding libraries from book bans appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts House passed a bill on June 26, 2026, protecting library materials from bans.
The bill empowers school librarians to select library materials without external interference.
Boston residents will benefit from increased access to diverse literature in schools and libraries.

articles.title: Federal judge in Boston halts Trump’s election executive order seeking to create a federal voter list
articles.description:
Plaintiffs argued in two lawsuits that Trump’s order should be found unconstitutional because the states and Congress, not the president, have the power to set election rules.

The post Federal judge in Boston halts Trump’s election executive order seeking to create a federal voter list appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Federal judge in Boston blocks Trump's executive order on federal voter list.
Plaintiffs argued the order violates states' rights to set election rules.
Decision impacts how Massachusetts and other states manage voter registration.
